ud7.5:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud7.5:1.3: And on that occasion Ven. Bhaddiya the Dwarf, following behind a large number of monks, was going to the Blessed One.
ud7.5:2.1:
From afar, the Blessed One saw Ven. Bhaddiya the Dwarf coming, following behind a large number of monks:
ugly, unsightly, stunted, treated with condescension by most of the monks.
ud7.5:2.2: On seeing him, the Blessed One addressed the monks,
ud7.5:3.1:
“Monks, do you see that monk coming from afar, following behind a large number of monks:
ugly, unsightly, stunted, treated with condescension by most of the monks?”
ud7.5:4.1:
“That, monks, is a monk of great power, great might.
The attainment already attained by that monk is not of a sort easily attained.
ud7.5:4.2:
And by means of it he has reached & remains in the supreme goal of the
holy life for which clansmen rightly go forth from home into homelessness,
knowing & realizing it for himself right in the here-&-now.”
ud7.5:5.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud7.5:6.4: one whose stream is cut, free from bonds.
